title: Document updated TUI observability cues
description: Refresh the TUI operator documentation so it describes the live status bar, in-progress elapsed timers, stale lease indicator, and current-cycle deferred visibility introduced by this feature.
acceptance_criteria: `docs/tui.md` documents the live status bar fields and updated bead-list observability cues.
Documentation reflects that deferred indicators are current-cycle-only rather than persistent across arbitrary future refreshes.
Documentation language matches the implemented TUI behavior and avoids describing unimplemented countdowns or persistent deferred state.

handoff_summary.completed: docs/tui.md updated with three targeted changes: (1) status bar description now shows the live `{N} running | {N} ready | {N} blocked | S:{mode}` format; (2) new "In-progress row indicators" subsection documents the elapsed timer `(Xm YYs)` and stale lease ` stale?` suffix elements; (3) new "Deferred bead indicator" subsection documents `⊘ deferred` as current-cycle-only.
handoff_summary.risks: None. All changes are documentation-only and align with the implemented behaviour in render.py, state.py, and app.py.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. docs/tui.md is not claimed by any sibling bead.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. docs/tui.md is not claimed by any sibling bead.

execution_history.summary: Updated docs/tui.md to document live status bar, in-progress elapsed timers, stale lease indicator, and current-cycle deferred bead marker.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
state.py was not in the original `expected_files` list, but the change is additive (new field only) with no risk to sibling beads modifying the same area.
No conflicts expected. Only `app.py` was modified; `state.py`'s `status_panel_text()` method is no longer called from `_update_status_panel()` but remains intact for any other callers.
Changes are isolated to `render.py`. The new `deferred_bead_ids` parameter is keyword-only with default `None`, so no existing callers break. No conflicts expected with sibling beads unless they also modify `render_tree_panel`.
